name: kimiflow
description: Calm context-aware Kimiflow launcher and active-session feature/fix loop for Codex with natural mode aliases, safe workspace preflight, red/green, autonomous recovery, code-review, memory, Obsidian, and Vault gates.

interface.shortDescription: Feature & bug-fix loop with adaptive discovery, autonomous local commits, project intelligence, repo docs, and findings
interface.longDescription: Kimiflow opens with a calm context-aware launcher showing one recommended action and compact project/memory/run health. It routes into full/grill/plan/build/quick/review/audit/fix modes, project mapping, memory/recall, Obsidian/Vault setup, local findings, backlog resume, feature work, fixes, improvement analysis, repo docs, or existing-feature review. Actionable feature runs inventory the workspace, batch only missing product facts, show a plain-language contract, and then continue autonomously unless a material product, authority, privacy, cost, external-access, or irreversible decision is required. Technical blockers and review findings change strategy inside the loop; verified named-path changes commit locally without another routine prompt, while push and release stay explicit. Grill, plan, review, and audit do not edit code until build authority or a slice selection exists. Active-session ownership supports safe parallel-session path checks and stale-state revalidation. Fixes preserve red/green evidence, project intelligence maps architecture/conventions/tests/flows, and a focused code-review ensemble plus mechanical Clarify, Discovery, plan, test, secret, and commit gates preserve quality without making the user review internal plans.
defaultPrompt: Open kimiflow and show project status
Run kimiflow full for this feature
Review this change with the kimiflow ensemble
Check an already-built feature with kimiflow
Map and understand this codebase with kimiflow
Refresh kimiflow project memory and recall
Open Obsidian MCP Terminal setup with kimiflow
Connect and check Obsidian memory health with kimiflow
Find architecture and refactoring opportunities with kimiflow
